question: Inside a Feelers template, what can appear between the double curly braces of an insert such as `{{ ... }}`?

options.text: Any valid FEEL expression; its evaluated value is inserted at that position.
Only a single variable name; no FEEL operators or function calls are permitted.
JavaScript expressions; FEEL is evaluated separately on form submission only.
Plain text only, with no expression evaluation at all.

a.text: The Variables/inserts section states you can use any valid FEEL expression within the double braces, with `{{if isAdmin then "an admin" else "a user"}}` as an example of a FEEL conditional in an insert.

b.text: Feelers inserts are not limited to bare variable names; the docs explicitly show a full FEEL `if ... then ... else ...` expression inside `{{ ... }}`.

c.text: Feelers does not use JavaScript; the docs say expressions inside double braces are FEEL.

d.text: The braces are evaluated, not treated as plain text; the docs show `{{username}}` being replaced by the variable's value.

explanation: The Variables/inserts section makes the rule explicit: anything inside `{{ ... }}` may be any valid FEEL expression. The worked example shows both a simple variable reference and a FEEL conditional in the same template line, confirming that the brace contents are evaluated as FEEL rather than treated as plain text or restricted to identifiers.

codeBlocks.stem: Hello {{username}}, you are {{if isAdmin then "an admin" else "a user"}}.
